stories.headline: Fuel prices rise for second straight week, diesel up by more than P3 a liter
stories.summary: Major oil companies including Shell, Seaoil, Petron, Petro Gazz and Unioil raised pump prices effective 6 a.m. Tuesday, July 7, with gasoline up by P0.20 to P0.25 per liter, diesel by P3.30, and kerosene by about P1.70 to P1.75. It is the second consecutive weekly increase across all fuel types.
stories.context: Year-to-date cumulative increases as of June 30 totaled about P45 per liter for gasoline, P32 for diesel and P29 for kerosene.

stories.headline: Marcos orders preemptive evacuations as Super Typhoon Bavi nears PAR
stories.summary: President Marcos directed government agencies to carry out preemptive evacuations, pre-position rescue teams and relief supplies, and enforce sea travel bans ahead of Super Typhoon Bavi. As of 2 a.m. Monday, PAGASA said the typhoon remained outside the Philippine Area of Responsibility with maximum sustained winds of 215 kph and gusts of 265 kph, and forecast it to enter PAR by midweek with a low chance of making landfall.
stories.context: Once inside PAR, the system will be given the local name Inday. PAGASA has said it may also enhance the southwest monsoon and bring widespread rain even if it does not make direct landfall.
stories.watch_for: PAGASA may raise wind signals over northern and eastern Luzon as early as Tuesday night or Wednesday morning if Bavi enters PAR as forecast.

stories.headline: PNP warns against misinformation, online incitement as Duterte impeachment trial opens
stories.summary: PNP chief Gen. Jose Melencio Nartatez Jr. warned the public against misinformation and online incitement to violence as the Senate opened the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte, directing the Anti-Cybercrime Group and intelligence units to strengthen round-the-clock monitoring of online activity and ordering local police commanders to validate and address viral false information in their areas.

stories.headline: Army declares Northern Samar's first district insurgency-free
stories.summary: The Philippine Army's 8th Infantry Division declared all 14 towns in Northern Samar's first legislative district free of New People's Army influence on July 4, citing a "Stable Internal Peace and Security Condition" reached through a combination of security operations, governance reforms and livelihood programs.
stories.context: The district covers Allen, Biri, Bobon, Capul, Catarman, Lavezares, Lope de Vega, Mondragon, Rosario, San Antonio, San Isidro, San Jose, San Vicente and Victoria.

stories.headline: BIFF-Bungos faction leader killed in Maguindanao del Norte raid
stories.summary: Esmael Abubakar, alias "Commander Bungos," chairman of the BIFF-Bungos faction of the Bangsamoro Islamic Freedom Fighters, was killed around 2 a.m. Sunday in Barangay Calsada, Sultan Kudarat, Maguindanao del Norte, after he allegedly opened fire on Army soldiers of the 601st Infantry Brigade and police who arrived to serve a court warrant against him.
stories.context: Abubakar had outstanding warrants for murder and arson, and the military attributed to his group a 2019 attack on soldiers and a bus-extortion campaign in the province.

stories.headline: Alex Eala's historic Wimbledon run ends in round of 16 loss to Paolini
stories.summary: Filipino tennis player Alex Eala lost to Italy's 13th-seeded Jasmine Paolini, 6-4, 4-6, 6-3, in the Wimbledon round of 16 on Centre Court, ending a run in which she had earlier upset defending champion Iga Swiatek. Eala, 21, became the first Filipina to reach the round of 16 at Wimbledon in the Open era.

stories.headline: Sandiganbayan orders arrest of Senator Marcoleta over P75-million plunder case
stories.summary: The Sandiganbayan's Third Division issued a warrant of arrest and a hold-departure order against Senator Rodante Marcoleta and three co-accused after denying his motion to quash a plunder case over an undeclared P75-million campaign donation from his 2025 senatorial run. Marcoleta was brought to Camp Crame for booking; his lawyer described his court appearance as a voluntary surrender, while the Interior Secretary said he was arrested under the warrant.
stories.context: Plunder is a non-bailable offense. The case stems from Marcoleta's own 2025 television admission that he received the funds from a former congressman and two businessmen.

stories.headline: Philippine stocks extend gains on easing inflation optimism
stories.summary: The Philippine Stock Exchange index rose 0.58%, or 35.96 points, to close at 6,223.99 on Monday, its fourth straight day of gains, as investors bought shares on expectations that June inflation eased and on falling global oil prices. Banking stocks led the advance while foreign investors were net sellers for the session.

stories.headline: China's submarine-launched ballistic missile test in the Pacific draws regional concern
stories.summary: China's navy conducted its first publicly acknowledged submarine-launched ballistic missile test into the Pacific Ocean on July 6, which Chinese state media described as routine training using a mock warhead. Japan had urged Beijing beforehand to reconsider the launch, and Japan, Australia and New Zealand all raised concerns afterward about its impact on regional stability.

stories.headline: Hamas says it will dissolve its Gaza governing authority
stories.summary: Hamas announced it would disband the Government Emergency Committee that has run civilian affairs in Gaza, describing the move as a concession meant to remove pretexts for continued Israeli military action and advance the current ceasefire process. A separate technocratic body, the National Committee for the Administration of Gaza, is expected to take over governance under a US-backed plan, though it has not yet entered Gaza pending Israeli approval.
stories.context: Hamas said the move does not mean it is giving up its political or military role, only direct civilian administration of Gaza.

stories.headline: Russian missile and drone strikes on Kyiv kill at least 20 on eve of NATO summit
stories.summary: Russia launched 68 missiles and 351 drones at Kyiv and surrounding areas overnight, killing at least 20 people and wounding dozens more, in the second large-scale attack on the Ukrainian capital in under a week. Russia's defense ministry said it targeted military-industrial and energy facilities in retaliation for Ukrainian strikes on Russian territory; Ukrainian officials said residential buildings were hit and appealed for stronger air-defense support.
stories.context: The attack came a day before NATO leaders were due to gather in Ankara, Turkey, where Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is scheduled to meet US President Donald Trump.

stories.headline: Iran holds mass funeral procession for slain Supreme Leader Khamenei
stories.summary: Iran held a 12-hour funeral procession through Tehran on July 6 for Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, who was killed at age 86 in an Israeli air strike on February 28 that also killed several family members, in the opening strike of the US-Israel war on Iran. Large crowds gathered at Tehran's Grand Mosalla and Imam Hussein Square for the ceremony, capping a multi-day mourning period.
stories.context: Khamenei's son Mojtaba, named as his successor, was notably absent from the funeral, reportedly due to injuries from the air strike and security concerns.
